text.title: rust-testing-unit Failure Modes
text.summary: Failure modes observed when applying the rust-testing-unit methodology. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Author tries to mock chrono::DateTime; mockall fails to compile or produces a useless mock.
antipattern.root-cause: mockall is for traits, not concrete types.
antipattern.fix: Wrap the concrete type behind a trait (Clock); mock the trait.

antipattern.symptom: Test calls spawn_blocking; passes locally; fails in CI on a multi-thread runtime.
Test uses tokio::time::sleep(50ms) to wait for a background task; flakes in CI.
antipattern.fix: Replace with tokio::time::pause + advance, or wait on a oneshot channel.

antipattern.symptom: Doc example on pub(crate) fn silently does not run; coverage shows 100%.
antipattern.root-cause: cargo test --doc only runs doctests on pub items.
antipattern.fix: Promote to a #[test], or make the function pub if a doctest is the right home.
